newsSince: Genuinely new since June 9: a binding Master Supply Agreement with CAPAC Energy establishing an exclusive long-duration-storage partnership in the DACH region through 2031 with a 750 MWh commitment and a pathway to 2 GWh. This is a new commercial proof point for the long-duration storage thesis. Stock +8.4% since June 9 but still -47.7% YTD.

sources.label: QuiverQuant: Eos secures $22.7M DOE loan advance
Eos Energy second DOE funding (investor relations)
Eos lands 750 MWh Germany (CAPAC) storage deal

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 9 on the long-duration-storage optionality this watchlist tracks. DOE funding/MOU items are older. -48% YTD reflects an unproven ramp; an +8.4% bounce does not change the test-time-scaling power-supply thesis. Hold rank 25.
verdicts.bull: Zinc LDES with DOE-backed capacity could supply firming for AI-driven grid load growth.
verdicts.neutral: Optionality only; execution and cash burn unresolved.
verdicts.bear: Down ~48% YTD; dilution risk and uncertain bookings-to-revenue conversion.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Project AMAZE line 2 ramp; MOU-to-order conversion
